data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Amazon uses a microservices architecture that enables independent service deployment and scaling.
data.technical_spec: Decoupled services designed using REST APIs, often with support for GraphQL for data retrieval.

data.title: Cloud-Based Infrastructure
data.description: Amazon's infrastructure is heavily reliant on AWS cloud services, providing scalability and flexibility.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes AWS services like EC2, S3, Lambda, and RDS for computing, storage, and serverless operations.

data.title: Highly Distributed Systems
data.description: Amazon's backend is a highly distributed system to ensure resilience and high availability.
data.technical_spec: Load balancers, caching layers with Amazon ElastiCache and CDN with CloudFront for distribution.

data.title: Service Oriented Architecture (SOA)
data.description: Adopts SOA to form a complex network of services that communicate over a network.
data.technical_spec: Services communicate via RPC and messaging protocols such as Amazon SQS and SNS.

data.title: Real-Time Data Processing
data.description: Processes vast amounts of data in real-time to provide recommendations and pricing updates.
data.technical_spec: Leverages AWS Kinesis and Apache Kafka for data streaming and processing.
